steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The word “preceding” establishes the sequence: the corona's brightness occurs before the flare. At the moment the brightness appears, the flare hasn't yet happened—it's about to.

steps.title: Apply the signal to the choices
steps.content: ”Impending” means “about to occur,” which precisely captures what the flare is when the corona shows brightness. The other choices fail because “antecedent” describes a reversed relationship, “innocuous” contradicts the destructive flares described earlier, and “perpetual” conflicts with the temporary, event-triggered nature of the phenomenon. This matches choice B — B is correct.
